name: OmniEstate
report: The _Check_reward function lacks proper input validation for the durations parameter. When the invest function is called with an end_date value other than 3, 6, 12, or 24, the duration[msg.sender] is left at its default value of 0. In _Check_reward, since none of the conditional branches (durations == 3/6/12/24) are satisfied when durations is 0, the function returns the uninitialized global variable total_percent, which retains its previous value—often a large reward amount from prior calculations. This allows an attacker to bypass intended staking duration logic and claim disproportionately high rewards by passing an invalid end_date during investment.
